target.title: test(agents): use neutral tool schema fixtures
target.body:
## Summary

- replaces reported plugin/tool fixture names with neutral synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` names in tests and expected diagnostics
- keeps this PR test-only and deliberately excludes the runtime-quarantine test files now owned by #88368
- preserves the same mocked unsupported-schema behavior while removing report-specific naming from reusable fixtures

## Landing note

This should land first in the schema-hardening train. It is a neutral fixture scrub only, and it now avoids the three runtime-quarantine test files touched by #88368 so #88491 -> #88368 -> #88493 can merge sequentially without test-file conflicts.

## Verification

- rebuilt the PR from current `origin/main` e1a98171417c into head a5f928f32ce, reducing the PR from +123/-127 across 14 files to +113/-117 across 11 files
- synthetic sequential merge proof for #88491 -> #88368 -> #88493 completed without conflicts using `git merge-tree --write-tree`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agents-models-skills.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`: 6 routed shards, 197 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` on the 11 touched files
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on the 11 touched files
- `git diff --check` plus touched-file scrub for reported fixture names, private local paths, and install-error output: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: passed on run `run_4c1cbf71c689`, lease `cbx_08acf130d404` / `swift-prawn` (provider=aws, command 9m47s, total 11m52s)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: test fixtures no longer preserve report-specific plugin/tool names in reusable runtime-schema diagnostics.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree based on current `origin/main` e1a98171417c, plus AWS Crabbox Linux proof for the changed gate on head a5f928f32ce.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebuilt the PR as a reduced single commit, ran targeted fixture tests, formatter check, targeted lint check, diff check, scrub, autoreview, synthetic sequential merge proof, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: touched tests and expected diagnostics use neutral synthetic names, and the first-wave PR sequence no longer conflicts on runtime-quarantine test files.

Observed result after fix: the scrub PR can land before runtime quarantine without dragging overlapping runtime test edits into the same landing step.

What was not tested: no product runtime behavior; this is intentionally tests-only fixture cleanup.
